Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **4/10** WARNING with **0** active confluences vs VIX Spike Tier **2/4** MEDIUM, **60%** probability, skew + backwardation both on
Cash signals say calm, options say pay up for protection. Trust the options market here. Backwardation plus active skew is a real bid for tails, and confluences lag the vol curve.
⚠ These two disagree: Regime State **41** TRANSITION, MIXED bias vs Macro Regime BROADENING at **30%** confidence
Broadening implies risk-on rotation while transition implies indecision. Trust the regime score. **30%** confidence on broadening is weak, and **^NDX -3.9%** does not look like healthy participation.
Today's tension
Options market is pricing a vol expansion the cash market refuses to confirm.
Bull
Crash Index sits at **4/10** with zero active confluences and the broadening macro regime favors small caps and ex-US, with **^KS11 +6.7%** leading Asia and **^RUT +0.4%** the only green US index.
Bear
VIX Spike Detector is on **MEDIUM** tier with **60%** probability, skew and backwardation both active, and **^NDX -3.9%** is bleeding while regime score sits at **41** in transition.
The tell: Watch whether VIX backwardation flips off or pulls in HYG and small-cap drawdown to form a T1 confluence. Either resolves the disagreement.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is split and the split is the trade. Crash Index reads 4/10 with no active confluences, yet the VIX Spike Detector is on MEDIUM with 60% odds and both skew and backwardation lit. You have calm prices and an options market quietly paying up for downside. Add a regime score of 41 in transition and a contrarian read at 42 in partial accumulate, and you get a market that wants to broaden but cannot get out of its own way while ^NDX -3.9% drags the US complex.

Position for the rotation, not the index. The leadership tape backs it: ^RUT +0.4% is the only green US print, ^KS11 +6.7% runs Asia, and ^STOXX50E +1.3% leads Europe. That is a textbook broadening setup with the Nasdaq as the funding leg. You lean long small caps and ex-US, fund it by trimming mega-cap tech, and you carry tail hedges because the vol surface is telling you they are cheap relative to realized calm.

The main risk is the disagreement resolves by price catching down to vol rather than vol bleeding into price. The reading that flips the stance is the activation of any T1 confluence (HYG, B2 small-cap drawdown, or JPY safe-haven flow). Zero confluences today is the only reason to stay constructive. One active confluence and you cut gross.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Regime reads BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is a weak signal pointing the right way. Broadening means leadership is widening out of the mega-cap complex into small caps, cyclicals, and ex-US, which the cross-region tape confirms with ^KS11 +6.7%, ^STOXX50E +1.3%, and ^RUT +0.4% while ^NDX -3.9% rolls.

The 30% confidence means do not bet the farm on it. You overweight small caps and international, underweight crowded mega-cap tech, and you keep position sizes modest because a transition regime score of 41 and contrarian 42 both say the market has not decided.

Confidence above 50% would let you press. Until then, lean, do not lunge.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

Cross-asset divergence sits in the ELEVATED zone with 46.5% of pairs diverging, 14 bearish against 6 bullish, and a Mother Jaws composite at 100. Divergence here means two assets that usually move together have stopped doing so.

Correlation drift is the speed of that breakup, the rolling correlation today minus where it sat recently. The loudest break is stlfsi4_d20 at +0.36 to -0.05, financial stress decoupling from its trend.

Then skew_vix at -0.34 to -0.84 (skew and VIX moving harder in opposite directions, classic protection bid), lqd_ief at -0.08 to +0.85 (IG credit and Treasuries suddenly fused, a duration trade), and t10y2y at +0.62 to -0.57 (the curve no longer tracking its own momentum). When this many anchor pairs slip at once, the macro narrative is mid-rewrite.

You reduce conviction on any single-factor trade until correlations re-anchor.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero T1 confluences are active and that is the cleanest bull tell on the page. The three watched pairings, VIX backwardation with HYG credit stress, with B2 small-cap drawdown, and with JPY safe-haven flow, are all dark.

Backwardation is firing on its own, but nothing in credit, small caps, or the yen is corroborating. That tells you the vol bid is positioning and hedging, not a fundamental break yet.

You can stay risk-on while this holds, lean into the broadening trade with small caps and ex-US, and keep the tail hedge on as cheap insurance. The instant any one of those three pairs activates, the disagreement between price and vol resolves the wrong way and you cut.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

Asia is leading the world and the US is the laggard, which sets up a difficult US open. ^KS11 +6.7% over 5 days is a powerful risk-on print, ^STOXX50E +1.3% confirms Europe is in line, and then you get to the US where ^RUT +0.4% is the only positive index and ^NDX -3.9% is the regional laggard.

Crypto is flat to soft with SOL-USD -0.2% and ETH-USD -2.8%, so the 24/7 risk proxy is not corroborating Asian strength. The lead-lag implies the US session opens with a rotation bid (small caps and cyclicals catching the global tape) and continued pressure on mega-cap tech.

You fade Nasdaq strength into the open and buy Russell weakness, with the Korea and Europe prints as your tell that the global impulse is real.

Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 4/10 · WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 4/10 and VIX Spike at MEDIUM with 60% probability is not a contradiction, it is a regime fingerprint. The Crash Index counts realized stress (credit, breadth, drawdowns, confluences) and finds 0 of the three T1 pairings active.

The VIX Spike Detector reads the options market directly, where skew is active and the VIX curve is in backwardation, meaning near-term vol is bid above longer-dated vol. Translation: nothing has broken in cash, and dealers and hedgers are paying premiums consistent with something about to.

You trust the options signal because it leads. Backwardation with active skew and no confluence is the window where tail hedges are still affordable.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 4/10 — a few early signals are stirring; watch whether more line up.
CRASH INDEX
4/10
WARNING — 3+ signals, active surveillance

Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).

Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).

Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (1)
• Dollar wrecking ball
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

VIX / Realized Vol 30dVolatility1.32-0.690.340Normal range
Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
